name: telemetry
description: Unified observability surface. Owns the THREE OpenTelemetry signals — traces, metrics, structured logs — under one boundary. Wraps `keepsuit/laravel-opentelemetry` for framework-native auto-instrumentation of HTTP, DB, queue, cache, view; wraps Monolog with a processor pipeline emitting newline-delimited JSON. Two sub-namespaces preserve the separation-of-concerns internally without splitting the module: `Academorix\Telemetry\Otel\*` for span + metric emission (via keepsuit's Tracer + Meter facades) and `Academorix\Telemetry\Logs\*` for the log record pipeline. Attribute-driven manual instrumentation (10 attributes: AsSpan / AsCounter / AsHistogram / AsGauge / AsUpDownCounter / AsContext / AsBaggage for OTel; AsLogContext / AsLogChannel / RedactedForLogs for logs). Fire-and-forget — no data stored locally; every signal exports via OTLP or a Monolog sink and the downstream backend (Grafana Cloud / DataDog / Honeycomb / Loki / self-hosted collector) owns retention. Trace-to-logs correlation is automatic: every log record picks up the active `trace_id` + `span_id` from the current OTel context.

opentelemetry.rationale: Laravel-native OTel wrapper. Turn-key auto-instrumentation of HTTP + DB + queue + cache + view via ~5 lines of provider glue instead of ~500 lines maintaining our own binding layer.

logs.rationale: Laravel's default Log facade IS Monolog. We register processors + a JSON formatter on top — no replacement of the framework logger.

traces.description: OpenTelemetry spans exported via OTLP. Auto-instruments HTTP server, HTTP client, DB queries, queue jobs; manual instrumentation via #[AsSpan] attribute.

cache.reason: High volume + noisy — opt-in per env.

view.reason: Only enable when investigating template render latency.

metrics.description: OpenTelemetry metric instruments (counters, histograms, gauges, up-down counters) exported via OTLP metrics.

logs.description: Structured JSON log records emitted through Monolog with a processor pipeline. Every record carries request_id + workspace_id + causer_id + trace_id + span_id.

processor_pipeline.name: PsrLogMessageProcessor
processor_pipeline.purpose: Standard Monolog processor. Handles PSR-3 interpolation.

processor_pipeline.name: IntrospectionProcessor
processor_pipeline.purpose: Adds source class + line + function to every record.

processor_pipeline.name: AcademorixContextProcessor
processor_pipeline.purpose: Injects academorix.request_id, academorix.workspace_id, academorix.causer_id from RequestContext.

processor_pipeline.name: OtelTraceContextProcessor
processor_pipeline.purpose: Adds trace_id + span_id from the active OTel context so log lines correlate with spans emitted by this same module.

processor_pipeline.name: ModuleContextProcessor
processor_pipeline.purpose: Evaluates every #[AsLogContext] enricher registered for the current call site via the compiled LogContextRegistry.

processor_pipeline.name: RedactorProcessor
processor_pipeline.purpose: Applies redaction rules to record fields. Class-level #[RedactedForLogs] marks specific fields; config-level rules cover global patterns.

processor_pipeline.name: JsonFormatter
processor_pipeline.purpose: Serialises to newline-delimited JSON. Loki, Grafana Alloy, DataDog agent all consume this format.

attribute_driven.description: Modules opt into custom instrumentation via attributes. Discovery pass at boot compiles the registries; runtime is vanilla method calls.

attributes.AsSpan: Wraps a method with a manual span. Params: name, kind, attributes[], record_exceptions, sampling_priority.
attributes.AsCounter: Emits +1 counter increment on method entry (or lifted from a param). Params: name, unit, amount, from, labels[].
attributes.AsHistogram: Records method duration_ms in a histogram. Params: name, buckets, unit, value, labels[].
attributes.AsGauge: Read-only observable gauge — the method's return value is the current reading. Called on scrape.
attributes.AsUpDownCounter: Emits a signed delta. Useful for concurrent-count tracking.
attributes.AsContext: Adds the method's parameter value to the current span attributes for the duration of the call.
attributes.AsBaggage: Propagates a key/value across service / queue boundaries via the W3C Baggage header.
attributes.AsLogContext: Marks a method as a log-record enricher — its return value is merged into `record.extra` for logs emitted from the declaring class.
attributes.AsLogChannel: Marks a class — all `Log::*` calls from this class route to the named Monolog channel.
attributes.RedactedForLogs: Marks a class or property — the marked fields are replaced with `[REDACTED]` before the record leaves the pipeline.

cross_signal_correlation.description: The single-module boundary is what makes trace-to-logs jump work end-to-end. The OtelTraceContextProcessor reads directly from the OTel context established by the OTel sub-provider — no cross-module boundary crossings.
cross_signal_correlation.propagation_across_queue_boundaries: TracePropagatingBusMiddleware serialises the OTel context into every dispatched job payload. On execution, listeners resume the trace so consumer spans link to producers; PropagateContextIntoJob restores workspace_id + causer_id into RequestContext so async-job log records carry the originating context.

central_admin.description: Debug + operational surface for the SRE team. Not workspace-facing.

migration_notes.rationale: Two modules were splitting one observability surface. Traces + metrics + logs are the three OTel signals — OTLP handles all three natively since spec v1.19. Merging removes duplicate providers/config/health probes and lets the trace-to-logs correlation processor live inside a single boot boundary.
